hypothesis: If a computational framework combining fragment-based polymer generation, DFT-refined force field parameters, multi-stage MD/GCMC simulations, and Bayesian optimization with pre-trained molecular representations is applied to the chemical space of hypothetical linear polyamines, then it will efficiently identify novel polyamine structures with higher solid-state direct CO2 capture performance than known reference materials within a limited simulation budget, because the surrogate-guided search exploits learned structure-property relationships to prioritize high-performing candidates without exhaustively simulating the entire chemical space.
